<> To replace one URL with another within a string in PHP, you can use the `str_replace` function. This function searches for a specific string and replaces it with another string. In this case, you want to replace the URL inside the anchor tag with a new one. Here is an example of how you can achieve this: ```php $string = "New post on the site http://stackoverflow.com/xyz1

"; $oldUrl = "http://stackoverflow.com/xyz1"; $newUrl = "http://yahoo.com/abc1"; $result = str_replace($oldUrl, $newUrl, $string); echo $result; ``` This code will output: ``` New post on the site http://yahoo.com/abc1

``` The `str_replace` function is efficient and works well for simple string replacements. It will replace all occurrences of the old URL with the new one in the string. A palindrome is a word, phrase, or sequence that reads the same backward as forward. An example of a palindrome is "madam" or "racecar". These types of strings are interesting in programming and can be used for various applications such as data validation and string manipulation.
